title: Pfizer Q1 Earnings Beat Expectations
description: Pfizer reported strong Q1 earnings, beating analyst expectations on revenue and EPS.
article_text:
Pfizer Inc. reported first-quarter earnings on Tuesday that beat Wall Street expectations across the board.

The pharmaceutical giant said net income rose 12% to $4.2 billion, or $0.74 per share. Analysts polled by FactSet had expected earnings of $0.68 per share.

Revenue climbed 8% to $13.7 billion, driven by strong demand for its vaccines and oncology portfolio, which offset a continued decline in COVID-19 product sales.

"We are very pleased with our strong start to 2026," said CEO Albert Bourla in a statement. "Our diversified portfolio and robust pipeline continue to deliver meaningful value for patients and shareholders."

The company raised its full-year guidance, now expecting adjusted earnings of $2.95 to $3.05 per share, up from the previous range of $2.80 to $2.95.

title: Eli Lilly Faces Mounjaro Patent Challenge
description: Generic drugmakers challenge key Mounjaro patent in federal court, setting up multi-year litigation.
article_text:
A coalition of generic drug manufacturers filed a patent challenge against Eli Lilly's blockbuster weight-loss drug Mounjaro in federal court on Monday.

The lawsuit, filed in the District of Delaware, claims that Lilly's patents covering the active ingredient tirzepatide are invalid and unenforceable due to prior art and obviousness.

Lilly said in a statement it would "vigorously defend" its intellectual property rights and was confident in the strength of its patents.

Legal analysts said the case is unlikely to be resolved before 2028 at the earliest, giving Lilly several more years of exclusivity even if the challenge ultimately succeeds.

title: J&J Drug Pricing Under Senate Scrutiny
description: Senate health committee grills J&J executives over US drug prices being multiples of what other countries pay.
article_text:
Johnson & Johnson executives faced pointed questions from a Senate health panel on Tuesday over the company's drug pricing practices in the United States.

Senator Bernie Sanders, who chairs the committee, accused J&J of charging American patients and insurers up to ten times more for drugs than patients in Canada, Germany, and Japan pay for the same medicines.

J&J's chief executive pushed back, arguing that high US prices fund the billions in research and development costs that make new medicines possible.

The hearing comes as Congress debates legislation that would allow Medicare to negotiate prices on a broader range of drugs than current law permits.

title: AbbVie Pipeline Update Impresses Analysts
description: AbbVie provides upbeat pipeline readout with multiple late-stage programs showing promise.
article_text:
AbbVie delivered a broadly positive pipeline update at its annual R&D day on Wednesday, with analysts pointing to strong data from its immunology and oncology programs as highlights.

The company said its next-generation immunology candidate had demonstrated efficacy comparable to its blockbuster drug Skyrizi in a head-to-head study, raising hopes that it could extend the company's dominance in the space well beyond the current patent cycle.

Shares rose 4% on the news, with several analysts upgrading their price targets.

"This is the pipeline update the market has been waiting for," wrote one analyst in a note to clients.

title: Merck Names New Chief Scientific Officer
description: Merck appoints Dr. Elena Vasquez as Chief Scientific Officer, succeeding Roger Perlmutter who held the role for a decade.
article_text:
Merck & Co. on Thursday named Dr. Elena Vasquez as its new Chief Scientific Officer, effective June 1.

Dr. Vasquez joins from Genentech, where she led oncology research for the past six years. She will oversee Merck's global research and development operations, which employ more than 17,000 scientists worldwide.

The appointment comes as Merck faces increasing pressure to fill its pipeline ahead of the 2028 patent expiration of Keytruda, its top-selling cancer drug that generated $25 billion in sales last year.

Current CSO Roger Perlmutter, who has held the role for ten years, will transition to a senior advisory position.

title: GSK Reaches Zantac Settlement Agreement
description: GSK agrees to pay $2.2 billion to settle thousands of lawsuits alleging its Zantac heartburn drug caused cancer.
article_text:
GlaxoSmithKline has agreed to pay $2.2 billion to settle approximately 80,000 lawsuits in the United States alleging that its former heartburn medication Zantac caused cancer, the company announced on Wednesday.

The settlement, which GSK said does not constitute an admission of liability, resolves the majority of claims filed against the company in state and federal courts.

Plaintiffs alleged that ranitidine, the active ingredient in Zantac, was unstable and could degrade into a probable carcinogen called NDMA under certain storage conditions.

GSK has consistently denied that Zantac caused cancer, and the company said it agreed to settle to avoid the uncertainty and expense of further litigation.
